steps.title: Use custom regression on the visible points
steps.content: The graph gives points such as $(-7,-2)$ and $(-5,-6)$ on the curve, and the model has the form $f(x)=\frac{a}{x+b}$. In Desmos, enter those two points as a table and run the custom regression $y_1\sim \frac{a}{x_1+b}$. Desmos gives $a=6$ and $b=4$, so $f(x)=\frac{6}{x+4}$.

steps.title: Apply the shift $g(x)=f(x+4)$
steps.content: The input shift replaces $x$ with $x+4$: $$g(x)=f(x+4)=\frac{6}{(x+4)+4}=\frac{6}{x+8}.$$ You can enter `g(x)=f(x+4)` in Desmos after defining $f$ to see the shifted graph.

steps.title: Apply the shift $g(x)=f(x+4)$ and match a choice
steps.content: Choice C is $g(x)=\frac{6}{x+8}$, so it matches the shifted function. C is correct.
